title: Staying Composed in High Pressure Moments
overview: Maintain composure under stress using breathing control, grounding cues, and stress simulations.
core_concepts:
1. Breath control triggers parasympathetic response. 
2. Physical anchors return attention to the present. 
3. Simulations build stress recovery capacity.
modifications: Shorten breath cycles; substitute visualization if physical anchor isn’t accessible.
coach_tip: Breathe first, then respond.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Demonstrate controlled breathing in stressful scenarios. - Reduce visible nervousness in observed drills. - Respond calmly to simulated pressure events.

protocols.steps: Inhale 3s, hold 3s, exhale 6s; repeat 5 cycles
Use a centering cue (touch fingertips or press feet to floor); take one slow breath
30s aerobic exercise → 30s See–Hear–Feel → composure task (e.g., mock Q&A)
protocols.expected_outcome: Improved transition from stress to focus

title: Command Commitment in the Moment
overview: Build decisiveness and all-in follow-through under uncertainty with physical anchors, mini challenges, and purpose alignment.
core_concepts:
1. Decisiveness is a skill trained through repetition. 
2. Mini challenges shrink hesitation windows. 
3. Purpose alignment sustains commitment.
modifications: Scale challenge difficulty up or down; use silent step if jumping is not suitable.
coach_tip: Treat commitment like a rep: decide, move, keep going.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Initiate action within 10 seconds of a decision point. - Reduce hesitation in uncomfortable tasks. - Report stronger connection to personal values when committing.

protocols.steps: Set a floor marker; inhale 3s, hold 3s, exhale 6s while saying “commit”; step across
Pull a random mini challenge from a jar; start within 10 seconds
Ask why this matters; note how it grows strength, connection, or meaning; proceed with 100% commitment

title: Release the Shock
overview: Absorb, label, and discharge the startle response so it does not immobilize action.
core_concepts:
1. Startle can trigger a freeze response. 
2. Labeling interrupts fear amplification. 
3. Movement and sensory expansion restore control.
modifications: Lower alarm volume for sensitivity; do seated versions.
coach_tip: Name it, move it, then return to the task.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Label and release shock within 10 seconds. - Shorten freeze duration in drills. - Return to task focus after a startle cue.

protocols.steps: Use a random alarm; pause 3s; label See–Hear–Feel; then 2–3 slow breaths
Shake hands, arms, and legs for 15s; breathe 3 rounds; repeat x3
Feet planted; explore See–Hear–Feel qualities (hard/soft, warm/cool, pleasant/unpleasant); soften resistance

title: Focus on the Positive
overview: Train the mind to generate adaptive appraisals in challenging scenarios to improve mood stability and performance.
core_concepts:
1. Cognitive appraisal shapes stress response. 
2. Positive imagery enhances motivation. 
3. Gratitude broadens attention and builds resilience.
modifications: Allow voice notes instead of writing; pair with a walk for activation.
coach_tip: Find the micro-win so your attention stays constructive.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Generate 3 positive outcomes for upcoming challenges within 2 minutes. - Increase daily positive affect reports. - Decrease rumination on setbacks.

protocols.steps: Identify a future challenge; write 3 plausible positive outcomes in 2 minutes
Visualize See–Hear–Feel of a successful outcome; identify the value (e.g., excellence, connection); choose one aligned action today
5-minute journaling on “What made it possible for me to be here today?”; list 5–10 items

title: Build Capacity for Uncertainty
overview: Increase tolerance for ambiguity by delaying closure, rehearsing uncertainty, and training recovery from disruptions.
core_concepts:
1. Uncertainty tolerance can be trained. 
2. Pendulation between safety and fear builds capacity. 
3. Micro disruptions teach flexible attention.
modifications: Shorten delay window initially; do seated versions for accessibility.
coach_tip: Stay with uncertainty long enough to learn from it.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Extend comfort with undecided states by 20 minutes. - Reduce urgency to close loops prematurely. - Recover focus within 60 seconds after deliberate disruption.

protocols.steps: Choose a low-stakes decision; delay 20 minutes; take 3 three-part breaths (belly, ribs, chest); relax resistance; note safety
Write 5 “what ifs”; pick one; imagine it for 2 minutes; pendulate attention between a safe body area and the fear site; breathe long and slow
Add a deliberate disruption to a routine task (switch order, random alarm); apply See–Hear–Feel to re-anchor

title: Command Outcomes When You Don’t Own the Room
overview: Create value without full control by training interruption recovery, attention in chaos, and strategic reframing.
core_concepts:
1. Influence is not control. 
2. Attention management under noise preserves clarity. 
3. Reframing defines winnable outcomes.
modifications: Use recorded ambient noise if a busy space isn’t available.
coach_tip: You may not control the room, but you always control your energy.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Recover talking flow within 10 seconds after interruptions. - Maintain task clarity in noisy environments for 5 minutes. - Identify at least one value-creating move when plans change.

protocols.steps: Practice speaking; when music interrupts, pause 10s; run See–Hear–Feel; continue
Sit in a busy space; sustain See–Hear–Feel for 5 minutes
Pick a scenario; ask “Given where this is going, how can I still create value?”; choose and execute one move

title: Concentration Power
overview: Strengthen focused and open attention to improve task performance and reduce distraction cost.
core_concepts:
1. Attention can be trained like a muscle. 
2. Alternating focused and open modes builds flexibility. 
3. Time-boxed sprints reduce switching costs.
modifications: Start with 60–90 seconds if needed; use earplugs for noise sensitivity.
coach_tip: Protect your attention like a resource.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Hold focused attention on an anchor for 2 minutes without switching. - Increase productive time via time-boxed sprints. - Reduce perceived distraction frequency.

protocols.steps: Choose an anchor (breath/object/mantra/sound); sustain attention for 2 minutes; when distracted, gently return
Sit comfortably; allow thoughts/images/sensations to pass; drop the urge to fix
Pick the next task; set a 25-minute timer; silence notifications; work solely on it; break and celebrate

title: Mental Reset Micro-Breaks for a Demanding Day
overview: Short, targeted resets to clear mental clutter and restore productive energy during heavy days.
core_concepts:
1. Brief state shifts restore cognitive resources. 
2. Sensory re-anchoring interrupts fatigue loops. 
3. Guided imagery and journaling clarify priorities.
modifications: Do seated/standing as needed; pair with a short walk if possible.
coach_tip: Reset before you force productivity.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Insert at least two micro-breaks on demanding days. - Report improved clarity post-reset. - Reduce error rate during long work blocks.

protocols.steps: Leave the task space; run See–Hear–Feel (far gaze, non-task sound, one tactile sensation)
Close eyes 1 minute; visualize a calm place; add See–Hear–Feel inside the scene; bring one quality back into work
5-minute brain dump; ask “What is the most important thing today?”; write the answer; revisit it

title: Better Breakfasts: Maximize Nutrition and Energy
overview: Use breakfast to stabilize energy, focus, and digestion through protein targeting, hydration, and balanced portions.
core_concepts:
1. Protein supports satiety and glycemic stability. 
2. Morning hydration improves mood and attention. 
3. Balanced macros regulate blood sugar and energy release.
modifications: Swap proteins (tofu, Greek yogurt, eggs, fish); use grab-and-go options (overnight oats, egg muffins, smoothies).
coach_tip: Build breakfast around protein first, then add balance.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Eat 20g protein at breakfast 5 days/week. - Report 30% fewer mid-morning energy crashes. - Reduce digestive discomfort by 25% over 4 weeks.

protocols.steps: Within 30 minutes of waking, include 20g protein; pair with fiber-rich carbs and healthy fats; log energy 2 hours later (1–10)
protocols.expected_outcome: Fewer pre-lunch cravings and steadier energy

protocols.steps: Drink 500 ml water upon waking; add electrolytes if training
Plate ½ fruit/veg, ¼ protein, ¼ whole grains; add small healthy fats; eat slowly and rate fullness at +15 min

title: Anti-Inflammatory Eating for Mood Stability
overview: Apply anti-inflammatory nutrition patterns to support mood stability through the day.
core_concepts:
1. Dietary patterns influence inflammatory markers linked to mood. 
2. Omega-3s, polyphenols, and fiber are protective. 
3. Reducing ultra-processed foods lowers inflammatory load.
modifications: For sensitive digestion, start with cooked plants and gradual fiber increases.
coach_tip: Choose foods that help your mood stay steadier over time.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Increase weekly servings of fatty fish/omega-3 sources. - Include polyphenol-rich plants daily. - Reduce ultra-processed foods to 3 servings/week.

protocols.steps: Plan 2–3 fatty fish meals/week or supplement per clinician guidance; add flax/chia/walnuts on non-fish days
protocols.prescription: Weekly meal planning plus daily additions

protocols.steps: Add two colors of plants at each meal (berries, leafy greens, herbs, legumes)
Identify top 3 trigger foods; swap each for a minimally processed option; track cravings and energy for 2 weeks

title: Eating for Cognitive Clarity
overview: Nutrition strategies to sharpen attention, working memory, and verbal fluency for high-stakes work.
core_concepts:
1. Stable blood sugar underpins cognitive performance. 
2. Hydration and electrolytes affect attention and mood. 
3. Specific nutrients support brain function.
modifications: Use a simple habit tracker (water, protein-first, post-meal walk).
coach_tip: Feed focus before you ask your brain to perform.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Reduce afternoon brain-fog incidents by 30%. - Improve post-meal focus ratings by 20%. - Hit daily hydration targets for 14 consecutive days.

protocols.steps: Prioritize protein and vegetables first at meals; add whole-grain carbs last; consider a 10-minute post-meal walk
Set a 2–2.5 L/day baseline; add 300–500 ml around workouts; include electrolytes when needed
Plan weekly: eggs for choline, leafy greens for folate, legumes for B-vitamins, fatty fish for omega-3

title: Walk with Command - Confidence in Heels
overview: Enhance body awareness, reduce the risk of injury, and create a smoother, more assured gait, essential for commanding presence in professional and social environments.
core_concepts:
1. Ankle and calf strength provide the foundation for confident heel-wearing. 
2. Single-leg balance enables smoother movement. 
3. Mind and body awareness, intentional alignment of posture and stride, projects confidence. 
4. Muscular endurance sustains comfort and control over long-duration events.
outcomes: - Improve ankle stability, demonstrated by holding a single-leg balance for 30 seconds without support. - Increase calf and ankle endurance, reducing fatigue when wearing heels for extended periods. - Report a 30% increase in perceived comfort when standing or walking in heels during meetings or events. - Demonstrate more fluid, confident gait with a 40% reduction in instability or the feeling of being unbalanced during movement in heels.

protocols.purpose: Strengthen the calves and Achilles tendon to stabilize the ankle when elevated in heels
protocols.steps: 1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, near a wall or chair for light support. 2. Rise up onto the balls of your feet, lifting heels as high as possible. 3. Hold for 2 seconds, then lower slowly. 4. Focus on engaging calves evenly and maintaining tall posture
protocols.prescription: 3 sets of 12–15 reps, 3x/week. Progress to single-leg raises when stronger

protocols.purpose: Improve balance and ankle proprioception for confident movement in heels
protocols.steps: 1. Stand tall and shift weight onto one foot. 2. Slightly elevate heel as if mimicking standing in heels. 3. Hold balance for 20–30 seconds, maintaining posture. 4. Switch sides. For added challenge, close eyes or move arms slowly

protocols.purpose: Integrate posture, core stability, and gait mechanics for walking smoothly and confidently in heels
protocols.steps: 1. Stand tall, shoulders back, core gently engaged. 2. Walk slowly across a room, placing heel down first, then rolling through the ball of the foot. 3. Focus on controlled, even strides with minimal sway. 4. Repeat for 5–7 minutes, maintaining presence and upright posture
protocols.prescription: Daily practice, particularly before important meetings or events

protocols.purpose: Build endurance in ankle stabilizers while engaging posterior chain, including posture muscles, glutes, and hamstrings, providing awareness for all-day confidence
protocols.steps: 1. Lie on the floor on your back with legs almost fully straight and feet hip-width apart, place the balls of the feet on the yoga block or step. 2. Lift heels off the ground, placing body weight into the yoga block or step as though standing in heels. 3. Extend arms back and into the ground and squeeze shoulder blades together. 4. Lift hips and glutes into the air to add resistance to the exercise. 5. Hold the elevated body position for 10–30 seconds, focusing on steady breathing. 6. Lower slowly

title: Embodying Authority Without Saying a Word
overview: Use posture and stance to carry yourself with authority, reduce nervous energy, and sustain physical presence throughout meetings and presentations.
core_concepts:
1. Body alignment signals confidence, energy, and leadership credibility. 
2. A grounded, upright posture increases stability and reduces nervous fidgeting. 
3. Proper stance reduces stress load on muscles, conserving energy for cognitive performance.
outcomes: - Demonstrate improved posture and stance in 80% of observed interactions. - Hold upright, confident posture for the duration of a meeting or presentation with perceived exertion 4 or less on Borg’s Scale. - Reduce fidgeting or nervous body language during speaking by 50%.

protocols.purpose: Train awareness of neutral posture and create a reliable reset before meetings
protocols.steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft. Roll shoulders up, back, and down, opening the chest. Imagine a string lifting from the crown of your head to elongate the spine. Breathe deeply, holding posture for 30 seconds
protocols.prescription: Repeat before every meeting or presentation

protocols.purpose: Reduce fidgeting and increase physical stability
protocols.steps: Place feet firmly on the ground, shoulder-width apart if standing; flat on the floor if seated. Shift weight evenly into both feet. On inhale, feel feet rooted; on exhale, release excess tension. Practice for 2 minutes before or during meetings
protocols.prescription: Use anytime nerves rise to anchor presence

protocols.purpose: Condition endurance for posture under stress
protocols.steps: Assume Power Stance (feet grounded, chest open, head tall, arms relaxed at sides). Hold posture for 2 minutes while practicing calm, steady breathing. Increase to 5 minutes over time. Rate effort on Borg’s Scale; should remain 4 or less
protocols.prescription: Train daily to build posture stamina for presentations

protocols.purpose: Strengthen spinal extensors and improve awareness of upright posture to reduce fatigue during long meetings or presentations
protocols.steps: Lie face down on a mat, arms by your sides, palms down. Engage your core gently by pulling the navel slightly toward the spine. On inhale, lift your chest a few inches off the floor while keeping your gaze down; neck neutral. Squeeze shoulder blades together, opening the chest. Hold for 5–10 seconds while breathing steadily. Slowly lower back down
protocols.prescription: Beginner: 2–3 sets, 3x/week. Progress to holding for 20–30 seconds per rep. Cue participants to feel the alignment between back muscle activation and upright posture

title: Train with Intention, Perform with Precision
overview: Boost safety and effectiveness of workouts, enhance movement confidence, and connect to your physical ability by tuning in to muscular activation and increasing proprioception.
core_concepts:
1. Build awareness of body position and movement to enhance control and safety. 
2. Emphasize quality and focus over speed or quantity. 
3. Use breathing patterns to stabilize posture and support efficient muscular activation. 
4. Prioritize correct technique and alignment to prevent injury and ensure effective training.
outcomes: - Higher quality reps, not just quantity. - Improved form and reduced injury risk. - Hold a single-leg balance for 30 seconds with proper form.

title: Communicate with impact
overview: Use clear, purposeful communication to create engagement, build trust, and drive action.
core_concepts:
1. Find your motivation so your passion is genuine. 
2. Make shared interest visible so others know why they should care. 
3. Say what you mean so your audience does not have to read between the lines.
coach_tip: Keep your message simple, direct, and outcome-focused.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Increased engagement during your presentations. - Your opinions and recommendations are clear and build confidence. - Action is taken as a result of your words.

title: Storytelling to influence decisions
overview: Use stories to make ideas memorable, create meaning, and help others see why a decision matters.
core_concepts:
1. Stories make ideas easier to remember. 
2. Relevance increases influence. 
3. Emotion helps people connect to the message.
coach_tip: Use a story to make the point feel real, not just logical.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - People remember the message more easily. - Your ideas feel more relevant and persuasive. - Decisions feel clearer and more human.

title: Mobilize a team
overview: Bring people into the process, remove barriers, and create shared ownership of the outcome.
core_concepts: 1. Engage all levels in solution and plan development; do not dictate. 2. Practice servant leadership and empower by removing barriers. 3. Celebrate incremental wins and reward positive leadership behaviours as much as results.
coach_tip: Build momentum by involving people early and often.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Team members have a sense of ownership and accountability. - Issues and blockers are surfaced quickly. - Engagement and productivity improve.

title: Cool the tension and put people at ease
overview: Create a calm, open tone so people feel safe, grounded, and able to engage constructively.
core_concepts:
1. Ease changes the tone of the room. 
2. Calm presence helps others settle. 
3. People respond better when tension is reduced.
coach_tip: Slow your pace and soften your tone before you try to solve anything.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - The room feels less tense. - People are more open and cooperative. - Your presence reduces friction.

title: Measured reactions in unexpected situations
overview: Stay grounded, scan the moment objectively, and choose a constructive response when something catches you off guard.
core_concepts:
1. Remain objective; feedback is fruitful, do not take it personally. 
2. Stay focused on what matters most. 
3. Know when to lean in and when to park it.
coach_tip: Pause first, then respond with intention.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - Heart rate and stress levels remain level during difficult conversations. - You bounce back quickly after challenging interactions. - Disruption is brief and closure is quick for all parties.

title: Make decisions with confidence
overview: Decide clearly under pressure and follow through without getting stuck in hesitation.
core_concepts:
1. Confidence grows through practice. 
2. Clear decisions reduce hesitation. 
3. Follow-through builds trust in yourself.
coach_tip: Decide on the next right step instead of waiting for perfect certainty.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - Decisions happen faster. - You spend less time second-guessing. - Follow-through becomes more consistent.

title: Increase your gravitational pull with colleagues
overview: Build presence that draws people in, strengthens trust, and makes collaboration feel easier.
core_concepts:
1. Presence influences connection. 
2. Consistency builds trust. 
3. People are drawn to clarity and steadiness.
coach_tip: Be steady, warm, and easy to read.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - People seek your input more often. - Collaboration feels easier. - Your presence builds trust.

title: Focus on what matters most
overview: Direct your attention toward what truly moves the needle and reduce distraction from low-value noise.
core_concepts:
1. Focus creates momentum. 
2. Not everything deserves attention. 
3. Clarity improves follow-through.
coach_tip: Keep asking, “What matters most right now?”
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - You spend more time on high-value priorities. - Distraction feels less controlling. - Follow-through improves.

title: Build your personal brand
overview: Present yourself with clarity and consistency so others understand your value and remember your voice.
core_concepts:
1. Repetition builds recognition. 
2. Clarity creates credibility. 
3. Your brand should reflect what you want to be known for.
coach_tip: Be consistent in how you show up, speak, and follow through.
time_to_result: Medium win (perform 3-6 times to see results)
outcomes: - People understand what you stand for. - Your message feels more consistent. - Your presence becomes more memorable.

title: Control the conversation
overview: Guide discussions toward clarity, outcomes, and mutual respect without becoming rigid or dominating.
core_concepts:
1. Know what motivates your audience. 
2. Facilitate, do not dictate, so defenses come down and the room stays open to your lead. 
3. Use bridging techniques to bring the conversation back from tangents.
coach_tip: Lead with structure, not force.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - You set and achieve specific meeting objectives. - People align on next steps. - Your key messages are remembered afterward.

title: Trust your interpersonal instincts
overview: Notice what is happening in the room and respond in a way that strengthens connection and credibility.
core_concepts:
1. Read the room. 
2. Trust what you sense. 
3. Use judgment and timing well.
coach_tip: Pay attention to the energy, not just the words.
time_to_result: Quick win (perform <3 times to see results)
outcomes: - You respond more naturally in conversations. - Your timing feels more intuitive. - Connection feels easier to build.

title: Prime the Message
overview: Clarify the message, outcome, and audience before an important conversation.
core_concepts:
1. Influence starts with clarity.
2. People move when the ask is simple.
3. Preparation reduces overexplaining.
modifications: Use voice notes instead of writing if needed.
coach_tip: Clear beats clever. Start with one point, one ask, and one reason it matters.
outcomes:
• Speak with a clear main point.
• State the desired outcome early.
• Reduce rambling in important conversations.

protocols.steps: Write the message in one sentence; cut extra words; say it out loud once
Finish the sentence “By the end of this conversation, I want…”; pick one outcome only
Note what matters most to the other person; adjust your first example to fit that priority

title: Fuel for Long Conversations
overview: Support steady energy and verbal clarity for meetings, pitches, and decision conversations.
core_concepts:
1. Stable energy supports better communication.
2. Hydration helps focus.
3. Light fueling beats showing up depleted.
modifications: Adjust food choices for dietary needs.
coach_tip: Do not try to influence on an empty tank.
outcomes:
• Fewer energy dips in long meetings.
• Better focus and verbal control.
• More steady presence through the day.

protocols.steps: Eat a protein-forward meal or snack before a long conversation; avoid arriving hungry
Drink water before the conversation; refill once during long sessions
Pair caffeine with food and set a cutoff time that protects sleep

title: Speak with Your Body
overview: Use posture, stance, and breath to support a more credible and confident delivery.
core_concepts:
1. Body language affects how messages land.
2. Posture shapes breathing and voice.
3. Small physical shifts change presence fast.
modifications: Can be done seated.
coach_tip: Your body speaks before you do.
outcomes:
• Stronger physical presence.
• More controlled voice and pace.
• Less visible stress in meetings.

protocols.steps: Stand or sit tall; plant feet; relax jaw and shoulders
Inhale through the nose; long exhale; speak on the next calm breath
Practice one minute of stillness with eye line up and shoulders open before a meeting

title: Stay Steady Through Pushback
overview: Handle resistance without getting reactive, defensive, or thrown off course.
core_concepts:
1. Pushback is information.
2. Regulation protects credibility.
3. Calm keeps the door open.
modifications: Use a written script for practice.
coach_tip: Pushback does not mean stop.
outcomes:
• Respond more calmly to resistance.
• Recover faster from challenge.
• Keep the conversation productive.

protocols.steps: Pause; take one slow breath; relax shoulders before answering
Name the concern you heard; identify one shared goal; answer from there
Lower your pace; shorten your answer; end with one clear next point

title: Build Buy-In Over Time
overview: Treat influence as a series of touches, not a one-time pitch.
core_concepts:
1. Big shifts take repetition.
2. Trust builds over time.
3. Follow-up is part of influence.
modifications: Use a note template if helpful.
coach_tip: Influence is built in layers.
outcomes:
• Better follow-up rhythm.
• Stronger relationship-based influence.
• More progress after initial conversations.

protocols.steps: Break one important influence goal into three touches: introduce, reinforce, follow up
After the conversation, send one simple recap with the ask or next step
Note what matters to the other person and one way to support that priority next time

title: Pause Before the Ask
overview: Reset emotions before important influence moments so delivery stays intentional.
core_concepts:
1. A short pause changes quality of response.
2. Regulation improves clarity.
3. Intentionality beats urgency.
modifications: Can be done silently.
coach_tip: A short pause protects quality.
outcomes:
• More intentional starts to hard conversations.
• Less emotional leakage.
• Better focus on the real ask.

protocols.steps: Stop for 60 seconds; breathe slowly; name the purpose of the conversation
Write one sentence starting with “What I do not need to carry into this conversation is…”
Decide how you want to sound: calm, clear, firm; say those words once before starting

title: Know Your Non-Negotiables
overview: Separate what must hold from what can flex in an important conversation.
core_concepts:
1. Clarity creates steadiness.
2. Flexibility is easier when boundaries are known.
3. Influence improves when priorities are explicit.
modifications: Use scripts if boundaries feel hard.
coach_tip: Know what must hold.
outcomes:
• Better boundary clarity.
• Faster choices in live conversation.
• Less overcompromising.

protocols.steps: Write one non-negotiable, one preference, and one trade you can make
Practice one simple sentence that states your boundary without apology
List two ways the plan could flex without losing the real outcome

title: Read the Room Fast
overview: Notice cues in tone, energy, timing, and attention so you can adjust without losing direction.
core_concepts:
1. Influence requires awareness.
2. Timing matters.
3. Adjustment is a strength, not a retreat.
modifications: Use 3 cue categories only if too much detail is overwhelming.
coach_tip: Reading the room helps it land better.
outcomes:
• Better real-time adjustment.
• Stronger situational awareness.
• More effective timing.

protocols.steps: Note energy level, attention, urgency, and openness in the room
Decide whether to push, pause, or simplify based on what you observe
After the meeting, note one cue you read well and one you missed

title: Follow Through Builds Influence
overview: Reputation grows when words and actions stay aligned after the meeting ends.
core_concepts:
1. Reliability builds trust.
2. Clear follow-through strengthens credibility.
3. Influence compounds over time.
modifications: Use calendar reminders.
coach_tip: Your word must mean something.
outcomes:
• Better reliability.
• Clearer next steps after meetings.
• Stronger reputation over time.

protocols.steps: Write the next step, owner, and date immediately after a key conversation
Send the follow-up when you said you would
Review open loops twice a week and close one

title: Protect Focus for High-Stakes Conversations
overview: Reduce noise and support attention before important conversations.
core_concepts:
1. Focus is easier when basics are handled.
2. Blood sugar and hydration affect attention.
3. Less noise improves message quality.
modifications: Adjust based on work context.
coach_tip: Protect attention before you need it.
outcomes:
• Better attention before key meetings.
• Fewer avoidable distractions.
• More mental steadiness.

protocols.steps: Eat, hydrate, and clear one distraction before the meeting starts
Silence notifications and keep one page of notes only
Use one grounding breath and restate your main point in six words or fewer

title: Name the Real Decision
overview: Define the decision clearly before spending energy on it.
core_concepts:
1. Clear questions improve decisions.
2. Vague problems create delay.
3. Naming the decision reduces noise.
modifications: Use voice notes if needed.
coach_tip: A good decision starts with the right question.
outcomes:
• Clearer decision framing.
• Less wasted time.
• Faster movement to action.

protocols.steps: Complete “The decision I need to make is…” in one sentence
protocols.prescription: At the start of a decision process

protocols.steps: Define what success looks like in one line
Name what this decision is not about so you do not solve three problems at once

title: Eat for Clear Thinking
overview: Use food and hydration to support alertness and faster thinking.
core_concepts:
1. Fuel affects cognition.
2. Stable energy supports better choices.
3. Light, balanced intake helps attention.
modifications: Adjust for dietary needs.
coach_tip: Clear thinking is easier when your brain is fed.
outcomes:
• Better focus for decisions.
• Fewer energy crashes.
• More consistent mental sharpness.

protocols.steps: Pair protein with fibre before long thinking blocks
Hydrate before caffeine and maintain water intake
Choose a lighter meal before important decisions and note how your energy responds

title: React Fast, Then Think
overview: Train the body to get comfortable choosing and moving quickly under pressure.
core_concepts:
1. Physical drills improve response speed.
2. Action reduces freeze.
3. The body can train decisiveness.
modifications: Use seated versions if needed.
coach_tip: Speed is trained.
outcomes:
• Faster response under pressure.
• Less hesitation.
• Better comfort with quick action.

protocols.steps: Use a simple reaction drill like left/right cue response for 60 seconds
Set a timer; choose between two options within 10 seconds; move immediately
Do 20 seconds of brisk movement then answer one short prompt fast

title: Decide and Name the Next Step
overview: Turn decisions into immediate action.
core_concepts:
1. Execution starts with a next step.
2. Delay grows when ownership is vague.
3. Small action protects momentum.
modifications: Use templates for action planning.
coach_tip: A decision is not complete until action starts.
outcomes:
• Faster start after decisions.
• Better follow-through.
• More visible momentum.

protocols.steps: After making the decision, write the next physical action
protocols.prescription: Every time a decision is made

protocols.steps: Name who owns the next step and by when
Make the first action take less than five minutes

title: Move Through Decision Discomfort
overview: Stay in motion when the answer is unclear or uncomfortable.
core_concepts:
1. Discomfort is part of deciding.
2. Avoidance grows with overthinking.
3. Smaller steps lower resistance.
modifications: Use shorter timers if needed.
coach_tip: You do not need perfect certainty to move.
outcomes:
• Less freezing when choices feel hard.
• Better tolerance for discomfort.
• More movement through uncertainty.

protocols.steps: Break the next move into one smaller action
Set a 5-minute timer and work only on the first step
Say: “I do not need perfect certainty to take the next step.” Then act

title: Use a Simple Decision Framework
overview: Sort options without overthinking.
core_concepts:
1. Frameworks reduce mental clutter.
2. Trade-offs become clearer when named.
3. Structure builds confidence.
modifications: Keep the framework to one page.
coach_tip: A simple structure beats spinning.
outcomes:
• Better structured decisions.
• Clearer trade-offs.
• Less circular thinking.

protocols.steps: List the decision, top 3 options, key risks, and likely upside
Use only three evaluation criteria and score each option quickly
Choose a review date after the decision instead of reopening it daily

title: Reduce Decision Fatigue
overview: Save mental energy for high-value decisions.
core_concepts:
1. Too many small choices drain focus.
2. Defaults protect energy.
3. Fewer trivial decisions improve better ones.
modifications: Use one default first, not ten.
coach_tip: Protect your brain for what matters most.
outcomes:
• More energy for important choices.
• Fewer low-value decisions.
• Better focus on priorities.

protocols.steps: Identify three repeat decisions you can simplify this week
Pre-decide one routine for meals, clothes, or planning blocks
Schedule your hardest decision work during your sharpest hours

title: Reset Before You Decide
overview: Use a brief reset so decisions are made from clarity, not stress.
core_concepts:
1. Stress narrows thinking.
2. Short resets improve perspective.
3. Clarity improves after regulation.
modifications: Can be done seated or walking.
coach_tip: Reset first.
outcomes:
• Better emotional control before deciding.
• More grounded choices.
• Less reactive decision-making.

protocols.steps: Step away for 60 seconds; take slow breaths; unclench jaw and shoulders
Ask: “What matters most here?” and write one answer
Decide whether this choice needs action now or after sleep

title: Lead the Decision Out Loud
overview: Communicate a decision clearly so others know the direction, owner, and measure of success.
core_concepts:
1. Clarity drives action.
2. Team execution needs explicit ownership.
3. Good decisions are easier to follow when well communicated.
modifications: Use a standard closing script.
coach_tip: Direction and ownership matter.
outcomes:
• Clearer decision communication.
• Better team execution.
• Stronger accountability.

protocols.steps: Say what has been decided in one sentence
Identify who owns the next step and by when
State how progress or success will be measured

title: Action Through a Physical Start
overview: Use movement to break hesitation and create a stronger action state.
core_concepts:
1. Movement changes state quickly.
2. The body can cue action.
3. A physical start helps beat inertia.
modifications: Use low-impact versions as needed.
coach_tip: Sometimes the body gets you moving first.
outcomes:
• Faster transition from thinking to doing.
• Lower avoidance.
• More energy at action start.

protocols.steps: Do 30-60 seconds of brisk movement before the first task
Say the task out loud and begin it immediately after the movement set
Repeat the same start cue for one week so the body learns the routine

title: Fuel the Moment
overview: Support steady energy, hydration, and focus before high-stakes interactions so presence does not drop when the moment gets real.
core_concepts:
1. Energy stability supports communication quality. 
2. Hydration helps attention and pace. 
3. Light fueling prevents crash-and-burn energy.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Fewer energy dips before meetings. - Better focus and verbal control. - More even presence through long days.

title: Speak with Your Body
overview: Use posture, stance, and breath to support a more credible and confident delivery in meetings, presentations, and introductions.
core_concepts:
1. Body language affects how messages land. 
2. Posture shapes breathing and voice. 
3. Small physical shifts change presence fast.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Stronger physical presence. - More controlled voice and pace. - Less visible stress in meetings.

title: Prime the Message
overview: Clarify the message, audience, and desired outcome before an important conversation so the point lands quickly and cleanly.
core_concepts:
1. Influence starts with clarity. 
2. People move when the ask is simple. 
3. Preparation reduces overexplaining.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Speak with a clearer main point. - State the desired outcome earlier. - Reduce rambling in important conversations.

title: Stay Steady Through Pushback
overview: Handle resistance without getting reactive, defensive, or thrown off course so the conversation stays productive.
core_concepts:
1. Pushback is information. 
2. Regulation protects credibility. 
3. Calm keeps the door open.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Respond more calmly to resistance. - Recover faster from challenge. - Keep the conversation productive.

title: Control the Conversation
overview: Guide discussions toward clarity, outcomes, and mutual respect without becoming rigid or dominating.
core_concepts:
1. Know what motivates your audience. 
2. Facilitate, do not dictate, so defenses come down. 3. Use bridging techniques to bring the conversation back from tangents.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Set and achieve specific meeting objectives. - People align on next steps. - Your key messages are remembered afterward.

title: Carry the Room
overview: Use posture, movement, and spatial awareness to enter a room with steadiness and visible confidence.
core_concepts:
1. Room entry shapes first impressions. 
2. Slow movement signals control. 
3. Awareness helps you claim space without force.
modifications: Short
coach_tip: No
outcomes: - Slower, steadier room entry. - More deliberate movement. - Stronger first impression in live settings.
